title: Jira-Faithful Frontend Overhaul
objective: Transform TaskFlow into a production-grade, Jira-faithful project management UI with drag-and-drop board, responsive design, error handling, loading states, and polished instant-feel performance.
evidence: Initial scaffold created; critical documents still require Plannotator gate approval.

artifact: dist/ — 670KB minified, 195KB gzipped, 0 TypeScript errors
Dev server started on port 3000 in 143ms
All 5 routes return HTTP 200
Route returns HTTP 200
Route returns HTTP 200
HTTP 200 after redirect
188KB gzipped (192,637 bytes)

check: Phase 1 complete — routing, error boundaries, skeletons, toasts
artifact: All 4 infrastructure components built and wired; 5 routes working
Installed tailwindcss@3 + postcss + autoprefixer + @tailwindcss/forms + @tailwindcss/typography + @tailwindcss/aspect-ratio + @tailwindcss/line-clamp
tailwind.config.js with jira-blue, jira-sidebar, jira-green, jira-red, jira-yellow, surface colors, Jira shadow scale
50KB CSS (9KB gzipped) + 672KB JS (195KB gzipped) = 204KB total gzipped
Type icon, key badge, priority icon, label chips, subtask progress bar, due date, avatar, hover left-border accent
bg-jira-sidebar (#0747A6), white text, active highlight, theme toggle, user avatar bottom
Breadcrumb nav (Projects / TaskFlow / Board), sprint dropdown, search bar, jira-blue Create button
Rounded containers, status-headers with counts, tighter spacing
Jira-matching priority icons (CRITICAL=priority_high red, HIGH=arrow_upward orange, MEDIUM=remove blue, LOW=arrow_downward green); Status badges with colored dots
IssueModal, CreateIssueModal, AddMemberModal, ErrorBoundary all use bg-jira-blue/hover:bg-jira-blue-hover
Circular with initials fallback, Jira-blue background for placeholder
@dnd-kit/core@6.3.1, @dnd-kit/utilities@3.2.2, ~14KB gzipped added
PointerSensor (8px activation), TouchSensor (250ms delay), KeyboardSensor configured in BoardView
DraggableCard wrapper with transform, opacity feedback, z-index lift
DroppableColumn with isOver blue ring highlight, column dim on drag
Rotated clone with drop shadow, 200ms ease-out drop animation
Removed draggable attribute and onDragStart from TaskCard; @dnd-kit handles all DnD
715KB JS (209KB gzipped) + 51KB CSS (9KB gzipped) = 218KB total, 0 TypeScript errors
HTTP 200 on :3000/board, 269ms startup

check: Added order field to Task type
artifact: types.ts: order?: number added to Task interface

check: Updated mock data with order values
artifact: All 8 tasks in constants.ts have order: TODO (1-3), IN_PROGRESS (1-2), REVIEW (1), DONE (1-2)

check: BoardView sorts by order per column
artifact: getTasksByStatus sorts by (a.order ?? 0) - (b.order ?? 0)

check: New tasks get next order in column
artifact: handleCreateTask assigns order = tasks.filter(sameStatus).length + 1

check: Status change reassigns order to end of target
artifact: handleUpdateTask auto-assigns maxOrder + 1 when status changes
0 TypeScript errors, 665ms build
@dnd-kit/sortable@10.0.0, ~5KB gzipped added
Each column wraps cards in SortableContext with task IDs; SortableCard uses useSortable with transform/transition
handleDragEnd: same status → arrayMove → onReorder(status, orderedIds) → sequential order assignment
handleDragEnd: different status → onTaskUpdate with explicit order at insert position

check: Drop on column droppable appends to end
artifact: handleDragEnd detects Status droppable, calls onTaskUpdate without explicit order → maxOrder+1
Better multi-container sorting — detects card corners for insertion between items
